_note: Curated list of common stops pilgrims add to their itinerary. Each entry has a stable id (used as the saved value when picked from the list), a display name, a city/category, and a short neutral description. Free-text entries are stored with id=null.

places.name: Quba Mosque
places.description: The first mosque in Islam. Sunnah to visit on Saturday morning.

places.name: Masjid Qiblatain
places.description: Where the qibla changed from Jerusalem to the Ka'bah.

places.name: Mount Uhud and the Martyrs' Cemetery
places.description: Site of the Battle of Uhud and the resting place of Hamza (RA) and 70 other martyrs.

places.name: Jannat al-Baqi
places.description: The Madinan cemetery, resting place of many Companions and family of the Prophet ﷺ. Open after Fajr and Asr for men.

places.name: Masjid al-Jumu'ah
places.description: Where the Prophet ﷺ led the first Friday prayer in Madinah.

places.name: The Seven Mosques (Sab'a Masajid)
places.description: Cluster of historic mosques near the site of the Battle of the Trench.

places.name: Wadi-e-Jinn (the magnetic valley)
places.description: Natural curiosity outside Madinah, popular with tour groups. Optional cultural visit.

places.name: Date market and farms
places.description: Madinah's famous date varieties (Ajwa, Anbara, Sukkari).

places.name: Jabal an-Nour (Cave of Hira)
places.description: Mountain where the first revelation came to the Prophet ﷺ. Visit is optional and not part of Hajj rites — climb is steep.

places.name: Jabal Thawr (Cave of Thawr)
places.description: Where the Prophet ﷺ and Abu Bakr (RA) sheltered during the Hijrah. Optional, not part of Hajj rites.

places.name: Jannat al-Mu'alla
places.description: Historic Makkan cemetery, including the grave of Khadijah (RA), the Prophet's first wife.

places.name: Birthplace of the Prophet ﷺ (Mawlid)
places.description: Now a library (Maktabah Makkah al-Mukarramah) near Marwah. Site significance only.

places.name: Masjid A'isha (at-Tan'eem)
places.description: Closest miqat to Makkah. Pilgrims commonly travel here to enter ihram for additional Umrahs.

places.name: Masjid al-Khayf
places.description: Historic mosque in Mina where the Prophet ﷺ prayed during Hajj.

places.name: Masjid Namirah (at Arafah)
places.description: Where the Prophet ﷺ delivered the Farewell Sermon. Visited on the Day of Arafah.

places.name: Masjid al-Mash'ar al-Haram
places.description: Mosque in Muzdalifah. Pilgrims pray Maghrib and Isha here on the night of the 9th of Dhul Hijjah.

places.name: Clock Tower (Abraj al-Bait) viewing
places.description: Museum and viewing deck overlooking the Haram. Optional cultural visit.

places.name: Operator-organised tour
places.description: Generic catch-all for whatever your group leader has planned.

places.name: Group meal
places.description: Iftar or dinner gathering with companions.

places.name: Shopping or errands
places.description: Souvenirs, clothing, gifts.

places.name: Rest at hotel
places.description: Recovery, especially during the days of Hajj.
